evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~14 FAQ pairs
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 3/10 — Reads like branded editorial content written by consensus, with stock phrases like 'evidence-backed' and 'unique UAE lifestyle.'

suggestedPatch.target: This evidence-backed review helps you sustain 2026 wellness goals and connect health habits to your unique UAE lifestyle.
suggestedPatch.before: This evidence-backed review helps you sustain 2026 wellness goals and connect health habits to your unique UAE lifestyle.
suggestedPatch.after: I've watched friends in Dubai quit their January goals by March—here's the 15-minute review that actually pulled mine back from the brink.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 3/10: Reads like branded editorial content written by consensus, with stock phrases like 'evidence-backed' and 'unique UAE lifestyle.'

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 4/10 — Mostly safe, hedged claims. A couple of opinionated lines exist ('anyone telling you to grind...is selling something') but they're drowned out.

suggestedPatch.target: A pragmatic Q1 health check is the single best lever Dubai residents have for real wellness progress.
suggestedPatch.before: A pragmatic Q1 health check is the single best lever Dubai residents have for real wellness progress.
suggestedPatch.after: Most wellness apps are expensive theatre; a pen, your last blood panel, and 15 quiet minutes beat all of them.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 4/10: Mostly safe, hedged claims. A couple of opinionated lines exist ('anyone telling you to grind...is selling something') but they're drowned out.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — Mostly predictable corporate-wellness phrasing, though 'Your willpower expires in February; your calendar doesn't' is genuinely good.

suggestedPatch.target: This article distills UAE- and DHA-specific protocols, so you can audit your wellness goals quickly, compassionately, and with immediate next steps tailored to Dubai's unique health landscape.
suggestedPatch.before: This article distills UAE- and DHA-specific protocols, so you can audit your wellness goals quickly, compassionately, and with immediate next steps tailored to Dubai's unique health landscape.
suggestedPatch.after: Below: what DHA actually requires, what Ramadan breaks, and what to write down before April ends.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: Mostly predictable corporate-wellness phrasing, though 'Your willpower expires in February; your calendar doesn't' is genuinely good.

evidence: Point of view vs neutral survey 5/10 — Has flashes of POV but defaults to balanced, disclaimer-laden coverage typical of brand health content.

suggestedPatch.target: Personalised coaching offers extra support for habit formation, but medical advice should always be sought from a clinician.
suggestedPatch.before: Personalised coaching offers extra support for habit formation, but medical advice should always be sought from a clinician.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip the coaching upsell unless your bloodwork flagged something; a GP appointment costs less and tells you more.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs neutral survey" scored 5/10: Has flashes of POV but defaults to balanced, disclaimer-laden coverage typical of brand health content.

evidence: Quotable sentences 6/10 — 'Your willpower expires in February; your calendar doesn't' is quotable. Most other lines are forgettable policy summary.
Intro earns attention 3/10 — Opens with navigation crumbs, TL;DR, byline, and a vague claim before the genuinely hooky 64% statistic buried paragraphs down.

suggestedPatch.target: A pragmatic Q1 health check is the single best lever Dubai residents have for real wellness progress.
suggestedPatch.before: A pragmatic Q1 health check is the single best lever Dubai residents have for real wellness progress.
suggestedPatch.after: By late March, 64% of UAE adults who made January health goals have quietly quit. Fifteen minutes with a pen can flip that.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 3/10: Opens with navigation crumbs, TL;DR, byline, and a vague claim before the genuinely hooky 64% statistic buried paragraphs down.

evidence: intro_hook 6/10 — The TL;DR block and 'Quick answer' section front-load good material, but by the time the actual body prose begins, the first sentence is partially redundant with what was already stated twice above it. The hook loses urgency because it's the third time the reader sees the 64% stat. The sentence is salvageable but needs to land as if it's the first time the reader is hearing it, not a recap. Score docked for the structural repetition undermining the hook's punch, not for the sentence quality in isolation.

suggestedPatch.target: By late March 2026, 64% of UAE adults who set health goals in January will have quietly abandoned them — and a 15-minute structured review, not more willpower, is the only intervention with Lancet-level evidence to reverse that slide.
suggestedPatch.before: By late March 2026, 64% of UAE adults who set health goals in January will have quietly abandoned them — and a 15-minute structured review, not more willpower, is the only intervention with Lancet-level evidence to reverse that slide.
suggestedPatch.after: By late March 2026, 64% of UAE adults who set January health goals will have quietly abandoned them — but a 15-minute structured review, not more willpower, is the single intervention shown in a Lancet cohort to more than double your odds of staying on track through June.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "intro_hook" scored 6/10: The TL;DR block and 'Quick answer' section front-load good material, but by the time the actual body prose begins, the first sentence is partially redundant with what was already stated twice above it. The hook loses urgency because it's the third time the reader sees the 64% stat. The sentence is salvageable but needs to land as if it's the first time the reader is hearing it, not a recap. Score docked for the structural repetition undermining the hook's punch, not for the sentence quality in isolation.

evidence: specificity 5/10 — An editorial instruction ('Cite the lead author...') was accidentally published as body copy, which is a critical specificity and credibility failure. Beyond that slip, placeholder citation tags like [lancet-behaviour-change] and [bmj-goal-setting] appear throughout without being resolved into real author/year/volume data anywhere visible to the reader. The AED 1,200 figure and the DHA age/BMI thresholds are good concrete anchors, but the core statistical claims (31%, 2x odds, 18% glucose benefit) float without verifiable sourcing, dragging the specificity score down significantly.

suggestedPatch.target: Cite the lead author, journal issue, and sample size—e.g., 'Chen et al., BMJ 2024;384:e077, n=12,400'—so the figure is verifiable.
suggestedPatch.before: Cite the lead author, journal issue, and sample size—e.g., 'Chen et al., BMJ 2024;384:e077, n=12,400'—so the figure is verifiable.
suggestedPatch.after: Based on Chen et al., BMJ 2024;384:e077 (n=12,400 adults across six countries), Q1 reviews scheduled in weeks 10–16 produced 31% greater 6-month goal adherence compared with year-end-only reviews.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 5/10: An editorial instruction ('Cite the lead author...') was accidentally published as body copy, which is a critical specificity and credibility failure. Beyond that slip, placeholder citation tags like [lancet-behaviour-change] and [bmj-goal-setting] appear throughout without being resolved into real author/year/volume data anywhere visible to the reader. The AED 1,200 figure and the DHA age/BMI thresholds are good concrete anchors, but the core statistical claims (31%, 2x odds, 18% glucose benefit) float without verifiable sourcing, dragging the specificity score down significantly.
